# WooCommerce 9.x — Tech Pack

## 1. Fingerprinting Signals

| Signal | Type | Value | Confidence |
|--------|------|-------|------------|
| `woocommerce` cookie present | Cookie | Name match | High |
| `wc-cart-hash` cookie present | Cookie | Name match | High |
| `is-woocommerce` CSS class on `<body>` | HTML class | `class="... is-woocommerce ..."` | High |
| `woocommerce-cart-nonce` in HTML | HTML substring | Nonce field in forms | High |
| Generator meta tag | HTML meta | `content="WooCommerce {version}"` | Version |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/` namespace in REST root | JSON response | Namespace present in `/wp-json/` | Definitive |
| `window.wc` JS global | JS global | Object present in page source | High |
| `window.woocommerce_params` JS global | JS global | Object present in inline scripts | High |
| `window.wc_add_to_cart_params` JS global | JS global | Object present in inline scripts | High |
| `woocommerce.min.js` script loaded | Script tag | Script src contains `woocommerce.min.js` | High |
| Wappalyzer result | MCP | `"WooCommerce"` | Definitive |

**Version extraction:**
```bash
# Extract WooCommerce version from generator meta tag:
curl -s {site} | grep -oP 'WooCommerce \K[\d.]+'

# Check REST API root for WC namespace and version:
curl -s {site}/wp-json/ | python3 -c "
import sys, json
data = json.load(sys.stdin)
ns = data.get('namespaces', [])
print([n for n in ns if n.startswith('wc')])
"

# Grep inline scripts for woocommerce_params version hint:
curl -s {site} | grep -o 'woocommerce_params[^<]*' | head -c 500
```

## 2. Default API Surfaces

| Endpoint | Method | Auth | Notes |
|----------|--------|------|-------|
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/` | GET | None | WC REST API root — lists all WC routes |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/products` | GET | None / Consumer Key | Public catalog; auth requirement configurable in WC settings |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/products/{id}` | GET | None / Consumer Key | Single product by ID |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/products/{id}/variations` | GET | Consumer Key | Product variations (nested endpoint) |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/products/categories` | GET | None / Consumer Key | Product category taxonomy |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/products/tags` | GET | None / Consumer Key | Product tags |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/products/attributes` | GET | None / Consumer Key | Product attributes (e.g. color, size) |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/orders` | GET / POST | Consumer Key (write scope) | Orders — requires auth; never public |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/orders/{id}` | GET / PUT | Consumer Key | Single order by ID |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/customers` | GET | Consumer Key | Customer list — requires auth |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/customers/{id}` | GET | Consumer Key | Single customer |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/coupons` | GET | Consumer Key | Coupon list — requires auth |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/shipping/zones` | GET | Consumer Key | Shipping zones |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/payment_gateways` | GET | Consumer Key | Payment gateway configuration |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/reports/sales` | GET | Consumer Key (read) | Sales summary report |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/system_status` | GET | Consumer Key (read) | System info, WC version, active plugins |
| `/wp-json/wc/store/v1/` | GET | None | Store API root — powers WooCommerce Blocks |
| `/wp-json/wc/store/v1/products` | GET | None | Store API product list |
| `/wp-json/wc/store/v1/products/{id}` | GET | None | Store API single product |
| `/wp-json/wc/store/v1/products/categories` | GET | None | Store API category list |
| `/wp-json/wc/store/v1/cart` | GET | Cookie / Nonce | Current cart state (session-bound) |
| `/wp-json/wc/store/v1/cart/add-item` | POST | `X-WC-Store-API-Nonce` | Add item to cart via Store API |
| `/wp-json/wc/store/v1/checkout` | POST | `X-WC-Store-API-Nonce` | Checkout submission via Store API |
| `/?wc-ajax=get_refreshed_fragments` | POST | Cookie | Legacy AJAX — returns cart fragment HTML |
| `/?wc-ajax=add_to_cart` | POST | Cookie | Legacy AJAX — add product to cart |
| `/?wc-ajax=apply_coupon` | POST | Cookie | Legacy AJAX — apply coupon code |
| `/?wc-ajax=remove_coupon` | POST | Cookie | Legacy AJAX — remove coupon |
| `/wp-admin/admin-ajax.php?action=woocommerce_*` | POST | Cookie / Nonce | Various WC AJAX actions (bypass WP REST) |

## 3. Config / Constants Locations

| Location | What's there | How to access |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| `wp-config.php` | DB credentials, WP table prefix, secret keys | Source only (no remote access) |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/system_status` | WC version, WP version, PHP version, active plugins, theme, currency, permalinks | Consumer Key GET |
| `window.woocommerce_params` | AJAX URL (`ajax_url`), currency symbol, currency position, decimal separator, nonce | JS eval / view page source |
| `window.wc_add_to_cart_params` | AJAX URL for add-to-cart, product URL, cart URL, i18n strings | JS eval / view page source |
| `window.wc_cart_fragments_params` | Fragment AJAX URL, cart hash URL, `wc_cart_hash` cookie name | JS eval / view page source |
| `window.wcSettings` | WooCommerce block (Gutenberg) settings — currency, locale, Store API nonce, site URL | JS eval / view page source |
| `window.wc.wcSettings` | Alias for Store API settings used by newer block-based checkout | JS eval / view page source |
| `woocommerce-cart-nonce` hidden input | Per-page nonce for cart operations | HTML scrape |
| WooCommerce Blocks inline data | `wp_localize_script` output embedded in HTML — contains Store API nonce | View page source / grep `wc-settings` |

## 4. Auth Patterns

| Pattern | Location | Notes |
|---------|----------|-------|
| Consumer Key + Secret — query params | `?consumer_key=ck_...&consumer_secret=cs_...` | Functional but not recommended (appears in logs) |
| Consumer Key + Secret — Basic auth | `Authorization: Basic base64(ck_...:cs_...)` | Preferred for REST API; generated in WC admin |
| WordPress Application Passwords | `Authorization: Basic base64(user:app-password)` | WP 5.6+; works for WP REST, not WC REST v3 natively |
| Store API nonce (`X-WC-Store-API-Nonce`) | `X-WC-Store-API-Nonce: {nonce}` request header | Required for Store API mutations (add to cart, checkout); obtained from `window.wcSettings.storeApiNonce` |
| Cookie-based cart session | `woocommerce_cart_hash`, `woocommerce_items_in_cart`, `wp_woocommerce_session_*` | Browser session; required for legacy AJAX cart endpoints |
| WP REST nonce (admin operations) | `X-WP-Nonce: {nonce}` request header | For WP admin REST calls; not WC Consumer Key |

**Consumer Key acquisition and usage:**
Consumer Keys are generated in WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API. They are
never embedded in public page source. If probing with read-only access, try the public
products endpoint without auth first — many stores leave it open.

**Store API nonce acquisition:**
```bash
# Nonce is embedded in page source as part of wcSettings inline script:
curl -s {site} | grep -o '"storeApiNonce":"[^"]*"' | head -1

# Or search the wc-settings script tag:
curl -s {site} | grep -A5 'id="wc-settings"' | grep -o '"nonce":"[^"]*"'
```

## 5. JS Bundle Patterns

| Path | Content |
|------|---------|
| `/wp-content/plugins/woocommerce/assets/js/frontend/woocommerce.min.js` | Core WC frontend — cart, checkout, form validation |
| `/wp-content/plugins/woocommerce/assets/js/frontend/add-to-cart.min.js` | Add-to-cart handler |
| `/wp-content/plugins/woocommerce/assets/js/frontend/cart.min.js` | Cart page JS |
| `/wp-content/plugins/woocommerce/assets/js/frontend/checkout.min.js` | Classic checkout JS |
| `/wp-content/plugins/woocommerce/assets/js/frontend/cart-fragments.min.js` | Cart fragment refresh (AJAX mini-cart) |
| `/wp-content/plugins/woocommerce/assets/client/blocks/` | WooCommerce Blocks (React-based cart/checkout) build artifacts |
| `/wp-content/plugins/woocommerce/assets/client/blocks/wc-blocks.js` | Main WooCommerce Blocks bundle |
| `/wp-content/plugins/woocommerce/assets/client/blocks/cart.js` | Block-based cart bundle |
| `/wp-content/plugins/woocommerce/assets/client/blocks/checkout.js` | Block-based checkout bundle |
| `/wp-content/plugins/woocommerce/assets/js/admin/` | WC admin panel JS (requires WP admin auth) |

Check each bundle URL + `.map` suffix for source maps. WC Blocks (React) bundles are
more likely to expose source maps than classic frontend JS.

## 6. Source Map Patterns

WooCommerce core JS files in production do **not** include source maps by default.
WooCommerce Blocks (React) may include source maps in some deployment configurations.

```bash
# Probe for source maps on WC Blocks bundles:
curl -I {site}/wp-content/plugins/woocommerce/assets/client/blocks/wc-blocks.js.map

# Check SourceMap response header on core WC JS:
curl -I {site}/wp-content/plugins/woocommerce/assets/js/frontend/woocommerce.min.js

# Custom WC themes/extensions compiled with webpack/vite may have maps:
curl -I {site}/wp-content/themes/{theme}/assets/js/woocommerce.js.map
```

WooCommerce child themes or custom plugins compiled from source (webpack, Vite, Rollup)
are the most likely source of exposed maps. Always check theme JS alongside plugin JS.

## 7. Common Plugins & Extensions

| Plugin | API it adds | Detection signal |
|--------|------------|-----------------|
| WooCommerce Subscriptions | `/wp-json/wc/v1/subscriptions`, `/wp-json/wc/v3/subscriptions` | `wcs_` prefixed cookies; `subscriptions` in HTML or JS |
| WooCommerce Bookings | `/wp-json/wc-bookings/v1/` | `wc-bookings` in HTML; booking calendar JS loaded |
| WooCommerce Memberships | `/wp-json/wc/v1/memberships` | `wc-memberships` in HTML/JS |
| WooCommerce Points & Rewards | No dedicated REST; hooks into cart | `wc_points_rewards` in HTML/JS globals |
| WooCommerce Stripe Gateway | No dedicated REST; adds Stripe.js | `js.stripe.com` script tag; `stripe` in inline JS |
| WooCommerce PayPal Payments | No dedicated REST; adds PayPal JS SDK | `paypal.com/sdk/js` script tag |
| YITH WooCommerce Wishlist | `/wp-json/yith/wcwl/` | `yith` in HTML/JS; `yith_wcwl_*` cookies or inline vars |
| YITH WooCommerce Ajax Wishlist | Legacy AJAX via admin-ajax.php | `yith_wcwl` action in AJAX calls |
| WooCommerce Product Bundles | Extends products REST with bundle_items | `bundle` in product type field |
| Storefront Theme | No API addition; theme detection | `storefront-woocommerce.css` or `storefront` in theme meta |
| WooFunnels / CartFlows | Funnel-specific checkout endpoints | `cartflows` or `woofunnels` in HTML/scripts |
| WooCommerce Multilingual (WPML) | Locale-aware product endpoints | `wpml_*` cookies; `lang=` param in API calls |

## 8. Known Public Data

| Endpoint | Data | Notes |
|----------|------|-------|
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/products?per_page=10` | Product list with IDs, names, prices, SKUs, categories, images | Auth may be required depending on WC "REST API" settings |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/products/categories?per_page=100` | Full category taxonomy with IDs, slugs, parent IDs, counts | Useful for building category maps |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/products/tags?per_page=100` | All product tags with IDs and slugs | — |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/products/attributes` | Attribute taxonomies (color, size, etc.) with IDs | Used to decode product variation structure |
| `/wp-json/wc/store/v1/products?per_page=10` | Store API product list — price, images, add-to-cart URL | Always public; designed for headless consumption |
| `/wp-json/wc/store/v1/products/categories` | Store API category list with slugs and counts | Always public |
| `/wp-json/wc/store/v1/cart` | Current cart (items, totals, fees, shipping) | Session-bound; no auth; reflects current browser session |
| `/wp-json/wc/v3/system_status` | Full environment report: WC version, WP version, PHP, plugins, theme | Requires Consumer Key; high-value target |
| `/?wc-ajax=get_refreshed_fragments` | Cart fragment HTML and `wc_cart_hash` | Always accessible; POST required |

## 9. Probe Checklist

Run these in order. Record result (✓ 200 / ✗ 403 / – 404) for each.

- [ ] `GET /wp-json/` — confirm `wc/v3` and `wc/store/v1` appear in the `namespaces` array
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wc/v3/` — WC REST API root; lists all routes with supported methods
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wc/v3/products?per_page=5` — confirm public product access (or note 401/403 auth gate)
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wc/v3/products/categories?per_page=100` — full category taxonomy with IDs
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wc/v3/products/tags?per_page=100` — tag list
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wc/v3/products/attributes` — attribute taxonomy (reveals variation structure)
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wc/store/v1/products` — Store API products (always public; try if v3 is gated)
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wc/store/v1/products/categories` — Store API categories
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wc/store/v1/cart` — Store API cart state (session-bound; reveals schema)
- [ ] `POST /?wc-ajax=get_refreshed_fragments` — legacy cart fragment AJAX; confirm AJAX endpoint active
- [ ] Check page source for `window.woocommerce_params` — capture `ajax_url`, currency symbol, nonce
- [ ] Check page source for `window.wcSettings` — capture Store API nonce and block configuration
- [ ] Check page source for `window.wc_add_to_cart_params` — cart URL, nonce
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wc/v3/system_status` (with Consumer Key if available) — full environment dump
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wc/v3/payment_gateways` (with Consumer Key) — active payment methods
- [ ] `GET /wp-json/wc/v3/shipping/zones` (with Consumer Key) — shipping configuration
- [ ] Probe `/wp-json/wc/v1/` and `/wp-json/wc/v2/` — confirm or deny older API versions still active
- [ ] Check for WC extension namespaces: `wc-bookings/v1`, `yith/wcwl/`, `wc/v1/subscriptions`

## 10. Gotchas

- **Products endpoint auth is configurable.** WooCommerce allows enabling/disabling public read access to products via REST under WooCommerce > Settings > Advanced > REST API. Many stores gate the REST API entirely; always try unauthenticated first, then fall back to the Store API (`wc/store/v1`) which is always public.

- **Store API is replacing legacy REST for headless.** The `wc/store/v1` namespace powers WooCommerce Blocks (block-based cart and checkout). It has no Consumer Key requirement. For reconnaissance of modern WC stores, probe `wc/store/v1` first — it leaks the same product and category data as `wc/v3` without auth.

- **Store API mutations require a nonce.** GET requests to `wc/store/v1` are public, but any state-changing request (add to cart, checkout) requires the `X-WC-Store-API-Nonce` header. The nonce is embedded in `window.wcSettings.storeApiNonce` — grep for it in the page source.

- **Consumer Key auth is distinct from WP auth.** WooCommerce Consumer Keys (`ck_` prefix) are not WordPress application passwords. They are only valid for the WC REST API (`wc/v3`). WordPress application passwords authenticate WP REST endpoints (`wp/v2`) but do not grant access to WC-specific resources.

- **Product variations are a nested endpoint.** Variation data is not included in the product list response by default. To retrieve all options for a variable product, you must hit `/wp-json/wc/v3/products/{id}/variations` separately. This doubles the request count for variable product enumeration.

- **Multiple WC API versions co-exist.** `wc/v1`, `wc/v2`, and `wc/v3` may all be active simultaneously. Always probe `v3` (current) first, but older versions sometimes expose fields or behaviors removed in `v3`. Specifically, `wc/v1/subscriptions` is the namespace for WooCommerce Subscriptions regardless of WC core version.

- **Legacy `wc-ajax` bypasses WP REST entirely.** Actions like `/?wc-ajax=add_to_cart` route through a WC-specific AJAX handler, not `wp-admin/admin-ajax.php` or the REST API. These endpoints are always active as long as WooCommerce is installed, even if the REST API is disabled.

- **Cart is session-bound.** The `wc/store/v1/cart` endpoint returns the cart for the current browser session (cookie-keyed). Automated probing without a persistent cookie jar will always see an empty cart. Use the `woocommerce_cart_hash` and `wp_woocommerce_session_*` cookies to maintain session state across requests.

- **EU/GDPR stores may hide price data.** Some WooCommerce configurations (or plugins like WooCommerce Price Based on Country) exclude or obscure pricing from the REST API for GDPR or tax compliance. A `200` response from the products endpoint with empty `price` fields is not a bug — it is an intentional store configuration.

- **`wc-cart-hash` cookie reveals cart activity.** The `wc-cart-hash` cookie contains an MD5 hash of the current cart contents. An empty cart yields a specific hash; a non-empty cart yields a different one. This can be used to detect whether a session has items without hitting the cart API.

## 11. GitHub Code Search Patterns

Use these queries on GitHub to find custom endpoints, plugin code, and configuration examples for this framework.

### Framework-Specific Queries

| Search Query | What it finds |
|--------------|---------------|
| `"wc/v3" language:php` | WooCommerce v3 REST API endpoints |
| `"register_rest_route" language:php path:woocommerce/` | WooCommerce custom REST routes |
| `"WC_Product" language:php` | WooCommerce product class usage |
| `"woocommerce_" language:php` | WooCommerce hooks and filters |

### Example Queries for WooCommerce

```bash
# Search for custom API routes/endpoints
site:github.com "WooCommerce" "api" filetype:php "wc/v3"

# Search for authentication patterns
site:github.com "WooCommerce" "auth" "consumer_key" language:php

# Search for configuration files with endpoint definitions
site:github.com "WooCommerce" "rest_api" "endpoint" language:php

# Search for custom post types, taxonomies, or extensions
site:github.com "WooCommerce" "product" "custom" language:php
```

## 12. Framework-Specific Google Dorks

Use these Google search queries to discover exposed endpoints, configuration files, and documentation for this framework.

### Discovery Queries

| Search Query | What it finds |
|--------------|---------------|
| `site:{domain} inurl:/wp-json/wc/v3/` | WooCommerce REST API v3 endpoints |
| `site:{domain} inurl:/my-account/` | WooCommerce customer account paths |
| `site:{domain} "woocommerce" "rest api"` | WooCommerce REST API references |
| `site:{domain} "wc_rest"` | WooCommerce REST API token patterns |

### Complete Dork List for WooCommerce

```
# API endpoints
site:{domain} inurl:/wp-json/wc/v3/
site:{domain} inurl:/wp-json/wc/store/v1/
site:{domain} inurl:?wc-ajax=

# Framework-specific paths
site:{domain} inurl:/my-account/
site:{domain} inurl:/cart/

# Configuration files
site:{domain} filetype:php "woocommerce" "version"
site:{domain} filetype:json "wcSettings"

# Documentation/leaks
site:{domain} "WooCommerce" "api" "endpoint"
site:{domain} "woocommerce" "consumer_key"

# Admin/debug paths
site:{domain} inurl:/wp-admin/
site:{domain} inurl:/my-account/
```

## 13. Cross-Cutting OSINT Patterns

These patterns apply across frameworks and should be checked for any detected technology.

### Favicon Hashing

Identify technology stack by hashing favicon and searching Shodan/Censys for same stack:

```bash
# Get favicon hash (mmh3 hash of favicon content)
curl -s "https://{domain}/favicon.ico" | python3 -c "
import sys, hashlib, base64
data = sys.stdin.buffer.read()
# Simple mmh3 hash simulation using Python
import mmh3 2>/dev/null || pip install mmh3
# Or use: python3 -c "import mmh3; print(mmh3.hash(data))"
print('Favicon hash needed for Shodan search: icon_hash')
"

# Search Shodan for same favicon (indicates shadow IT subdomains)
# site:shodan.io search: icon_hash:{hash}
```

**What it reveals:** Hidden subdomains running same framework stack as main site.

### Source Map Discovery

Check for source maps across all JS bundles:

```bash
# Extract all JS bundle URLs from HTML
curl -s "https://{domain}/" | grep -oP 'src="[^"]+\.js[^"]*"' | grep -oP '"[^"]+' | tr -d '"' > js_urls.txt

# Check each for .map file
while read url; do
  map_url="${url}.map"
  status=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "${map_url}")
  [ "$status" = "200" ] && echo "SOURCE MAP: ${map_url}"
done < js_urls.txt
```

**Build tool patterns:**
| Build Tool | Source Map Pattern | Detection |
|------------|-------------------|------------|
| Webpack | `{bundle}.js.map` or `//# sourceMappingURL=` | Check response header `X-SourceMap` |
| Vite | `{name}-[hash].js.map` | Vite manifest `manifest.json` |
| Rollup | `{bundle}.js.map` | Check `sourceMappingURL` comment |
| esbuild | `{bundle}.js.map` | Check `sourceMappingURL` comment |
| Next.js | `/_next/static/chunks/*.js.map` | Only if `productionBrowserSourceMaps: true` |

### Tech Stack → API Pattern Mapping

Auto-map detected frameworks to likely endpoint patterns:

| Framework | Common API Patterns |
|-----------|---------------------|
| Next.js | `/api/*`, `/_next/data/*`, `/api/auth/*`, `/api/trpc/*` |
| WordPress | `/wp-json/*`, `/wp-json/wp/v2/*`, `/wp-admin/admin-ajax.php` |
| Shopify | `/api/2024-10/graphql.json`, `/products.json`, `/collections.json` |
| Rails | `/api/v1/*`, `/assets/*`, `/users/sign_in` |
| Laravel | `/api/*`, `/livewire/message/*`, `/sanctum/csrf-cookie` |
| Strapi | `/api/*`, `/admin/*`, `/api/upload*` |
| Magento | `/rest/V1/*`, `/pub/static/*` |
| Django | `/api/*`, `/admin/*`, `/accounts/*` |

When Phase 3 detects a framework, use this table to prioritize Phase 5/6/7 probes.

### Email Naming Convention Analysis

Extract emails from theHarvester/GitHub results to predict internal subdomains:

```bash
# Sample emails found: john.doe@example.com, jane.smith@example.com
# Predicted subdomains: mail.example.com, smtp.example.com, exchange.example.com

# Common patterns:
# first.last@ → internal.example.com, mail.example.com
# firstinitial+last@ → owa.example.com, outlook.example.com
```

**Add to Phase 9 session brief:** Note email patterns and predicted subdomains.
